article_meta.title: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell on June 25, 2025, in Washington, DC

reasoning: The model does a generally good job identifying genuine bias in the author's own prose. It correctly avoids flagging quoted text (Powell's quotes, Navarro's quotes, Gotlob's quotes). Most flagged instances are defensible. However, there are some questionable items and a few missed instances.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: The model correctly avoids flagging any quoted text. All flagged items come from the author's own connecting prose. Zero attribution violations.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Most items are defensible. The item about 'Trump has asked for more...' is somewhat questionable - this is relatively neutral paraphrasing of Trump's actual stated positions. The 'Economists, academics and former Fed officials broadly agree' item is defensible as slant. The 'forthcoming about the renovation' item is a good catch. The 'remarkable show of defiance' and 'gloves are off' items are strong catches. The 'even more shocking' item is correct. The 'It enables the Fed to do its core mission' item is reasonable but could be seen as widely accepted economic principle rather than opinion.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model missed some instances: 'stunning two-minute video' contains a subjective qualifying adjective; 'Trump's relentless pressure campaign' uses loaded language; 'ridding the Fed of its independence could backfire spectacularly on Trump' presents opinion as fact; 'Among the many problems with Trump's demands' frames Trump's position negatively. The 'ardent defender' characterization was also missed.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Labels are mostly appropriate. 'Spin' is correctly applied to dramatizing language. 'Opinion Statements Presented as Fact' for the Fed effectiveness claim is defensible. 'Slant' for the selective emphasis on expert agreement and Fed transparency is appropriate. The Trump 'asked for more' item labeled as Spin is debatable - could also be Word Choice or might not be bias at all.
